name: Pupukea-Paumalu Trail (Ted Makalena)
tagline: A mellow North Shore forest walk through ironwoods and native plants — a breather between surf sessions.
parking: Free parking at the trailhead on Pupukea Road across from the Boy Scout Camp
bestTime: Any time — it's shaded and low-key
talkStory: About 2 miles of interconnected loop trails with minimal elevation change through a mix of ironwood, Cook pine, and native Hawaiian plants. This is a community-maintained trail system that's great for a casual nature walk, trail running, or mountain biking. Don't expect epic views — this is more of a forest bathing experience. Perfect for North Shore visitors who want a break from the beach. The trails can be confusing without a map — download the trail guide from Na Ala Hele before you go.
